title: Email notifications arriving hours late
body: Task assignment notifications used to arrive within a minute or two. For the past week, they've been arriving 2-4 hours late. This is causing people to miss deadlines because they don't know they've been assigned a task until hours later. It seems to be worse in the morning — notifications sent around 9-10am take the longest to arrive. Afternoon notifications are usually faster but still delayed.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed report — the morning pattern you noticed is a really useful clue.

You mentioned this started about a week ago and that notifications used to arrive promptly. Can you think of anything that changed around that time? For example, did your team grow, did you start using a new feature, or did you notice any other changes in how TaskFlow was behaving? I'm trying to understand what the environment looked like right before this started happening.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Hmm, let me think... I don't think our team size changed. But now that you mention it, I think around that time we started getting these daily digest emails from TaskFlow — like a summary of project activity or something marketing-related. They show up in the morning. I'm not sure if that's related but the timing does line up with when the delays are worst. Other than that I can't think of anything obvious that changed.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's a really interesting connection — the timing of those daily digest emails lining up with the worst delays is worth exploring.

Can you tell me more about what happens when the delayed notifications do finally arrive? For instance, do they tend to show up all at once in a batch, or do they trickle in one by one over those 2-4 hours? And is this happening for everyone on your team, or have you only confirmed it from your own inbox? I'm trying to get a sense of whether something is holding all the emails back and then releasing them, versus each one being individually slow.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: They seem to trickle in over time rather than all showing up at once. Like I'll get one, then another 20 minutes later, then another one after that. It's not like they're all held up and then released together. And yeah, I've heard the same complaint from a couple other people on my team — it's not just me. One of my reports actually missed a deadline yesterday because she didn't see the assignment notification until like 3 hours after I assigned it to her.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Task assignment email notifications delayed 2-4 hours, likely due to new daily digest emails saturating the send queue","problem":"Task assignment notifications that previously arrived within 1-2 minutes are now delayed by 2-4 hours. The delays are worst in the morning (9-10am) and affect multiple team members, causing missed deadlines.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The daily digest email feature, introduced approximately one week ago, is sending a large volume of emails through the same queue/pipeline as transactional task notifications. The digest runs in the morning, flooding the queue and causing task notifications to wait behind digest emails. Notifications trickle out as the queue slowly drains rather than arriving in a batch, consistent with a rate-limited or serialized email sender processing a backlogged queue.","reproduction_steps":["Assign a task to a user around 9-10am when daily digest emails are being sent","Observe that the assignment notification email is delayed by 2-4 hours","Repeat the same assignment in the afternoon and observe a shorter (but still present) delay","Check the email sending queue depth during morning hours vs. afternoon"],"environment":"Affects multiple users on at least one team. Started approximately one week ago, coinciding with the introduction of daily digest emails. Not limited to a single email provider or user.","severity":"high","impact":"Team members are missing task assignment deadlines because they don't learn about assignments for hours. At least one confirmed missed deadline. Affects the core value proposition of task notifications.","recommended_fix":"1. Inspect the email sending infrastructure — confirm whether digest and transactional emails share a queue. 2. Separate transactional notifications (task assignments, deadline reminders) into a high-priority queue distinct from bulk/marketing sends like digests. 3. If a single queue must be used, implement priority levels so transactional emails skip ahead of digest batches. 4. Consider rate-limiting or staggering the digest send to avoid a morning spike.","proposed_test_case":"Send a batch of N digest emails followed by a task assignment notification. Verify the assignment notification is delivered within the SLA (e.g., 2 minutes) regardless of digest queue depth. Repeat at various digest volumes to confirm the priority mechanism holds under load.","information_gaps":["Exact email infrastructure architecture (queue system, rate limits, provider)","Volume of daily digest emails being sent and how many users/teams receive them","Whether the delay affects teams or organizations beyond the reporter's team","Whether other transactional emails (e.g., deadline reminders, comment notifications) are also delayed"]}
